topic: Why we get goosebumps when cold (and during music)
caption: Ever wondered why you get goosebumps when it's cold outside or when your favorite song comes on? 🎵 It's not just about being chilly or emotional - there's actual science behind it! 💡 Did you know that goosebumps are caused by the contraction of tiny muscles at the base of each hair follicle? 💁‍♀️ This phenomenon is triggered by the release of adrenaline, a hormone that prepares our body for 'fight or flight'. 🏃‍♀️ But what's fascinating is that this same response can be triggered by strong emotions, like the thrill of listening to music that gives you chills! 🎶 Save this so you don't lose it. Share with someone who needs to see this. Follow @healthrecode for daily science-backed health. Don't forget - your future self will thank you! 💕
